commands.review_summary:
The PR prefixes node-proxied browser HTTP error messages with their HTTP status and updates browser proxy tests for stale-target error classification.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +20. Total +25 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main drops the HTTP status in runBrowserProxyCommand while the existing stale-target classifier requires `404:` plus `tab not found`. I did not run the full remote-node Chrome repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof or maintainer override is still needed; there is no narrow code repair for automation to make on this PR.

target.title: fix(browser): preserve HTTP status in node-proxied browser errors
target.body:
## Summary

- The node browser proxy (`runBrowserProxyCommand` in `extensions/browser/src/node-host/invoke-browser.ts`) collapsed a `>= 400` browser-route response into `new Error(<body.error>)`, **dropping the HTTP status**.
- That error crosses the `node.invoke` boundary as a plain **string** (Error properties are not preserved over the RPC), so the gateway's stale-target retry classifier — `isChromeStaleTargetError` in `browser-tool.actions.ts`, which requires `msg.includes("404:") && msg.includes("tab not found")` — **never matches** a node-proxied `tab not found`. The existing drop-`targetId` retry never fires and the stale-`targetId` error surfaces to the agent.
- This PR **preserves the status in the message** (`"404: tab not found …"`, `"403: action targetId must match request targetId"`) so the gateway's existing classification/retry works through the node proxy — restoring parity with the local browser path. It is a pure formatting change in the `>= 400` branch; validation and timeout error paths are untouched.

Out of scope (follow-up): the `403 "targetId must match request targetId"` case is now correctly **classifiable** but still has no retry consumer. Adding a retry there is a separate request-normalization concern, intentionally not bundled here.

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior or issue addressed
On a gateway with a **remote browser node**, agents intermittently receive `tab not found` (404) or `action targetId must match request targetId` (403) from the browser tool when a cached `targetId` goes stale (tab closed / target rotated). The gateway already ships a drop-`targetId` retry for the stale-tab case, but it is gated on the surfaced error carrying the HTTP status token (`404:`). Because the node proxy strips the status, that retry never fires for **remote-node** browsers, and the error reaches the agent.

### Real environment tested
- **Before fix** — observed live on an OpenClaw gateway **v2026.5.27** with a remote macOS Chrome node reached via `browser.proxy` (the relevant code on `main` is unchanged from v5.27).
- **After fix** — the `@openclaw/browser-plugin` package test suite, which exercises the real `runBrowserProxyCommand` code path.

### Exact commands run after this patch
```
node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/browser/src/node-host/invoke-browser.test.ts
```

### Evidence — before fix (live gateway log, remote node; conn/id redacted)
```
⇄ res ✗ node.invoke 116ms errorCode=UNAVAILABLE errorMessage=INVALID_REQUEST: Error: action targetId must match request targetId conn=… id=…
⇄ res ✗ node.invoke 115ms errorCode=UNAVAILABLE errorMessage=INVALID_REQUEST: Error: action targetId must match request targetId conn=… id=…
# and the 404 variant from the same class:
⇄ res ✗ node.invoke … errorMessage=INVALID_REQUEST: Error: tab not found: browser tab "…"
```
The surfaced messages carry **no `<status>:` token**, so `isChromeStaleTargetError` returns `false` and no retry is attempted.

### Evidence — after fix (test output)
```
 RUN  v4.1.7
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  14 passed (14)
```
New / updated assertions in `invoke-browser.test.ts`:
- a `500` proxy error now surfaces as **`"500: tab not found"`** (status preserved), and
- a `404 {error: "tab not found: …"}` now surfaces as **`"404: tab not found …"`** — i.e. it now carries both `404:` and `tab not found`, the exact tokens `isChromeStaleTargetError` requires, so the existing drop-`targetId` retry will fire.

### Observed result after fix
Node-proxied browser errors retain their HTTP status, restoring the gateway's stale-target classification/retry for **remote** browser nodes (parity with the local browser path).

### Evidence — after fix (live run of the shipped gateway classifier)

Beyond the package tests above, I ran the **actual shipped gateway classifier** against the before/after node-proxied error strings on a live gateway. `isChromeStaleTargetError` was extracted **verbatim** (brace-matched) from the installed production build's `plugin-service-*.js` and `eval`'d, then fed the `>= 400` proxy output as formatted by the **shipped** logic (status dropped) vs **this patch** (status preserved):

```
404 tab-not-found (stale targetId):
  BEFORE (shipped):  Error("tab not found: …")        isChromeStaleTargetError("user", …) -> false   (retry NOT fired — the bug)
  AFTER  (patched):  Error("404: tab not found: …")    isChromeStaleTargetError("user", …) -> true    (drop-targetId retry now FIRES)

403 targetId-mismatch:
  BEFORE (shipped):  Error("action targetId must match request targetId")          -> false
  AFTER  (patched):  Error("403: action targetId must match request targetId")      -> false   (status now preserved/classifiable; 404-retry intentionally not consumed — see Out of scope)

RESULT: PASS — fix restores the gateway stale-target retry for node-proxied 404s; 403 unchanged.
node v22.22.0 | classifier sourced verbatim from /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw (v2026.5.27)
```

The shipped classifier body that gated this (verbatim from the installed build):

```js
function isChromeStaleTargetError(profile, err) {
  if (!profile) return false;
  if (profile === "user") {
    const msg = String(err);
    return msg.includes("404:") && msg.includes("tab not found");
  }
  // …non-"user" profiles resolve browser config; the default path returns on the gate above
}
```

This exercises the decisive half end-to-end with real shipped code: the gateway's retry classifier was returning `false` for node-proxied `tab not found` (status stripped), and returns `true` once this patch preserves the status — so the existing drop-`targetId` retry now fires for remote-node browsers.

### What was not tested in this PR
The patched `runBrowserProxyCommand` was not executed inside a full browser-node process against real Chrome with the production gateway↔node RPC hop. The patched **producer** output is covered by the package vitest (the real `runBrowserProxyCommand` code path); the **consumer** (the shipped gateway `isChromeStaleTargetError`) is run live above against the real before/after strings. A full gateway + remote-node live-browser capture can follow on request.
